Distinct architectural commitment from passenger eVTOL: prioritizes payload, range, and short-strip operation over hover endurance.
propulsion_details: Four electric lift propellers and one cruise propeller; autonomous fixed-wing eVTOL designed for short-strip / cargo delivery operations; lithium-ion battery.
sources: Pyka technical white papers 2020–2024.
FAA Part 137 / Special Conditions filings 2023.
Pyka press releases including 2022-06-21 Pelican unveil.

control_architecture: autonomous flight; NASA UTM-integrated; vertical takeoff, pitch-over transition, cruise, vertical landing with modular pod delivery
creator: Bell Textron
disclosure_citation: Bell APT (Autonomous Pod Transport) publicly disclosed August 2018; APT 70 (70 lb / ~32 kg payload variant) first flight 2019-08; demonstrated package delivery and NASA UTM integration trials 2020-2021. Documented in Bell Textron press materials, NASA UTM program reports, and FAA filings.
ip_citations: Bell Textron APT patent estate, multiple US filings 2017-2024
prior_art_notes: Bell APT (Autonomous Pod Transport) establishes US prior art for the modular-cargo-pod tail-sitter eVTOL — a reusable quad-rotor tail-sitter airframe carrying a swappable cargo pod mounted between the rotor booms. Establishes prior art for: (1) modular swappable cargo pod + reusable propulsion airframe architecture in tail-sitter form, (2) NASA UTM-integrated autonomous cargo eVTOL operations (2020-2021 trials), (3) the pod-as-deliverable design pattern. Together with airbus-popup (modular passenger capsule), elroy-air-chaparral (modular cargo pod), and the academic modular-aerial-robot lineage, places modular-pod eVTOL architecture in commercial prior art from 2017-2018 forward.
propulsion_details: Tail-sitting quad-rotor airframe with a swappable cargo pod ('pod transport') that mounts between the four rotor booms; the airframe takes off vertically, transitions to horizontal cruise, and delivers the pod. The modular pod is the deliverable; the propulsion airframe is reusable. Battery-electric. Distinct from lift+cruise designs by the tail-sitter transition and the modular-pod payload concept.
sources: Bell Textron press releases on APT, 2018-2021.
NASA UAS Traffic Management (UTM) program reports including Bell APT trials.
FAA Part 107 / BVLOS waiver records for Bell APT operations.

creator: Jetson AB (Italy / Sweden joint operation)

Establishes Northern European prior-art lineage for consumer-grade ultralight eVTOL with open-rotor architecture and rotor-failure reconfiguration.

Jetson ONE technical specifications, jetsonaero.com.
FAA Part 103 ultralight registration records (Jetson ONE customer aircraft).

disclosure_citation: LIFT Aircraft Hexa publicly unveiled 2018-10-25; first crewed flights 2019; FAA Part 103 ultralight category (no pilot license required); public 'flight experiences' / training-flight programme launched 2021-2024. Documented in LIFT Aircraft press materials, FAA Part 103 registrations, and U.S. Air Force AFWERX Agility Prime contracts.

Establishes prior art for: (1) per-rotor-independent battery/motor architecture with deep failure tolerance, (2) consumer-grade single-joystick SVO for an 18-rotor eVTOL, (3) Part 103 ultralight commercial flight-experience deployment. Operates under FAA Part 103 ultralight rules. A maximally-redundant single-seat multirotor eVTOL.
sources: LIFT Aircraft press releases and FAA Part 103 registrations 2018-2024.
U.S. Air Force AFWERX Agility Prime contract records (LIFT Hexa).

creator: Pegasus Universal Aerospace (Johannesburg, South Africa)

ip_citations: Pegasus Universal Aerospace patent estate, South African and PCT filings
prior_art_notes: Pegasus Vertical Business Jet is South Africa's lead commercial VTOL design — distinct architectural commitment to executive-jet operations (2,200 km range, 400 kt cruise) with VTOL hover capability. Establishes African continental prior-art lineage for commercial VTOL business aviation distinct from urban air taxi. Architecturally similar to Lockheed Martin / Aurora LightningStrike (XV-24A, US military 2016) in fuselage-internal lift fan + cruise jet topology.
propulsion_details: Twin internal lift fans embedded in fuselage for hover (vertical lift); twin cruise jet engines mounted on rear empennage for horizontal flight; integrated hybrid-electric architecture. Combines executive-jet form factor with VTOL capability.
sources: Pegasus Universal Aerospace press releases 2018–2024.
South African Civil Aviation Authority engagement records.
Aero South Africa exhibition materials 2018-11-08.

creator: SkyDrive Inc
disclosure_citation: SkyDrive SD-03 (precursor demonstrator) public manned flight 2020-08-25 at Toyota Test Field; SD-05 production design unveiled 2021-09; Japan Civil Aviation Bureau type-certification application accepted 2021-10. SkyDrive founded 2018-12 (incorporation date), spinning out from CARTIVATOR volunteer eVTOL project (founded 2012).
ip_citations: SkyDrive Japan patent filings, JP and PCT applications 2018–2024
prior_art_notes: SkyDrive SD-05 is Japan's lead passenger eVTOL, with Japan Civil Aviation Bureau type-certification application accepted 2021-10. The 12-motor coaxial-pair architecture is similar to EHang EH216-S; the program establishes Japanese prior-art lineage for multirotor passenger eVTOL and the JCAB SC-VTOL-equivalent certification approach. Originated in the volunteer CARTIVATOR project (2012), one of the earliest organized open-development eVTOL efforts.
propulsion_details: Twelve coaxial-pair lift motors mounted on six arms (six coaxial pairs); lithium-ion battery pack.
sources: SkyDrive Inc press releases 2018–2024.
Japan Civil Aviation Bureau type-certification application docket.
Toyota Motor Corporation investor disclosures (Toyota is a SkyDrive backer).

control_architecture: triplex fly-by-wire; coordinated nacelle tilt and prop-pitch transition control; simplified vehicle operations (SVO) intent
creator: Joby Aviation
disclosure_citation: Joby S4 unveiling and first flight: Joby first publicly disclosed S4 prototype 2018-12-20 (Joby press release); first untethered transition flight 2017-08 (predecessor S2); S4 Production Prototype first transition flight reported in Joby Aviation S-1 SEC filing 2020-08, full disclosure in 10-K filings 2021–2025.
ip_citations: US 10,994,841 (Bevirt et al., 'Aircraft hover stability')
US 11,377,217 (Bevirt et al., 'Vertical take-off and landing aircraft')
Multiple additional Joby patents in the 11,xxx,xxx series
prior_art_notes: The Joby S4 is the lead commercial tilt-rotor electric eVTOL. Direct architectural descendant of bell-xv-15 (1977) and v-22-osprey (1989) — Joby's tilt geometry, conversion corridor, and gimbaled prop-rotor topology are classical tilt-rotor architecture with electric distributed propulsion substituted for cross-shafted turboshaft. Establishes prior art for: (1) six-rotor tilt-rotor with stop-in-cruise props, (2) BLDC direct-drive tilt-rotor propulsion, (3) Part 23 SVO certification basis. Joby's patent estate (US 10,994,841 et seq.) covers specific implementations but is anticipated on the architectural level by XV-15 and V-22 disclosures.
propulsion_details: Six 5-blade variable-pitch tiltable propellers powered by independent BLDC motors; four wing-mounted (tilting) and two stabilator-mounted (tilting); each propeller stops in cruise to reduce drag.
sources: Joby Aviation S-1 (2020-08) and 10-K filings 2021–2025, SEC EDGAR.
Bevirt, JoeBen et al. US Patent 10,994,841 and US Patent 11,377,217.
Joby Aviation press releases 2018-12-20 (S4 unveil) and subsequent.
Vertical Flight Society Forum technical papers from Joby engineering staff 2019–2024.

creator: teTra Aviation Corporation (Tokyo, Japan) / Tasuku Nakai
disclosure_citation: teTra Aviation Corporation (founded 2018 in Tokyo, an outgrowth of GoFly Prize-competing students) revealed the Mk-3 personal eVTOL (2020, GoFly Prize 'Pratt & Whitney Disruptor Award' winner) and subsequently the production Mk-5 single-seat personal eVTOL (2021); first crewed flights and FAA Special Airworthiness Certificate process underway. Documented in teTra materials and GoFly Prize records.
ip_citations: teTra Aviation patent estate, Japanese / US filings 2018-2024
prior_art_notes: teTra Mk-5 establishes Japanese prior art for the high-rotor-count single-seat personal eVTOL — a fixed-wing airframe with 32 small distributed lift rotors for hover redundancy plus wing lift for cruise efficiency. A GoFly Prize-derived design. Establishes prior art for: (1) very-high-rotor-count (~32) personal eVTOL with fixed wings, (2) the GoFly Prize lineage of personal-flight designs. Together with skydrive-sd-05 (JP, multirotor air taxi), aska-a5 (JP/US, drive+fly), honda-evtol (JP, hybrid), and the Japanese fictional VTOL anchors, deepens Japan's eVTOL footprint. Together with lift-aircraft-hexa (US, 18-rotor) and ehang-eh216 (CN, 16-rotor), places high-rotor-count personal/small eVTOL architecture in prior art.
propulsion_details: A single-seat personal eVTOL with a large number of small distributed lift rotors (the Mk-5 uses 32 fixed-pitch electric rotors mounted on tandem fixed wings — a 'fixed-wing-with-many-distributed-lift-rotors' design for hover redundancy and cruise efficiency); lithium-ion battery; designed for the personal-recreational segment. The Japanese entry in the high-rotor-count personal eVTOL space.
sources: teTra Aviation Mk-3 / Mk-5 technical materials 2019-2024.
GoFly Prize competition records (teTra Mk-3, Pratt & Whitney Disruptor Award).

creator: Bellwether Industries (London, United Kingdom)
disclosure_citation: Bellwether Industries (founded 2019 in London by Kai-Hsiang Lin) publicly disclosed the Volar — a four-seat ducted-fan flying-car concept designed to look like a luxury car — through 2021-2024 design and sub-scale-prototype releases. The 'Antelope' 1/6-scale demonstrator made tethered flights 2021. Documented in Bellwether materials.
ip_citations: Bellwether Industries Volar patent estate, UK / EU / PCT filings 2019-2024
prior_art_notes: Bellwether Industries' Volar concept (UK, 2019-) establishes prior art for the 'luxury-car-aesthetic flying car with internal ducted fans' — eight electric ducted lift fans buried inside a sleek closed-body vehicle with no exposed rotors, smooth aerodynamic surface in cruise. Distinct from Alef Model A (rotors under mesh skin, whole-body rotation) and from open-rotor multirotor eVTOLs. Adds UK depth to the flying-car / ducted-fan-array prior-art base alongside Lilium (DE), Aurora LightningStrike (US), Bell X-22 (US), Bell Nexus (US), Doak VZ-4 (US), Pegasus VBJ (ZA), Boeing Phantom Swift (US), and Horizon Aircraft Cavorite X5 (CA).
propulsion_details: Eight electric ducted lift fans buried inside a sleek closed-body vehicle (no exposed rotors) — the lift fans take air through louvered intakes and exit through louvered outlets, with the body surface staying smooth in cruise; intended hybrid-electric powertrain. The 'luxury-car-aesthetic flying car with internal ducted fans' design — distinct architecturally from the open-rotor multirotor and tilt-rotor eVTOL families. UK design lineage.
sources: Bellwether Industries Volar / Antelope technical materials 2019-2024.

creator: Bell (Textron Inc)
disclosure_citation: Bell Nexus design publicly unveiled 2019-01-07 at CES Las Vegas; Nexus 4EX revised four-rotor variant unveiled 2020-01-07. Program wound down circa 2022; design materials and filings remain in the public record.
ip_citations: Bell / Textron Nexus patent estate, multiple US filings 2017–2022
prior_art_notes: Bell Nexus is one of the Uber-Elevate-era US passenger eVTOL concepts (2019). Although the program wound down, the public design disclosures remain prior art for: (1) hybrid-electric tilting-ducted-fan eVTOL architecture, (2) Bell's specific tilt-duct geometry distinct from Joby's open-rotor tilt-rotor approach. Establishes additional US prior-art lineage for tilt-duct passenger eVTOL (with bell-x-22 from 1966).
propulsion_details: Four (originally six) tilting ducted lift fans driven by a hybrid-electric powerplant (turbine-genset + battery + electric motors). Lateral wingtip and aft-mounted ducted-fan geometry.
sources: Bell / Textron press releases 2019–2022.
CES 2019 and CES 2020 Bell Nexus unveilings.
Bell Nexus patent filings, USPTO records.

creator: Elroy Air Inc (San Francisco)
disclosure_citation: Elroy Air founded 2016 by David Merrill and Kofi Asante; sub-scale Chaparral demonstrator publicly unveiled 2019-01-30; full-scale C1 first hover flight 2023-11-12 at Camarillo, CA. Air Force AFWERX / USAF Agility Prime contracts 2021-09 onward.
ip_citations: Elroy Air patent estate, multiple US and PCT filings
prior_art_notes: Elroy Air Chaparral establishes prior art for: (1) hybrid-electric (turbine-genset + battery) cargo eVTOL, distinct from pure-battery designs by extending range to 500+ km, (2) modular swappable cargo-pod architecture decoupled from aircraft turnaround. Hybrid powerplant anticipates similar claims on series-hybrid eVTOL propulsion topology.
propulsion_details: Eight lift fans (four fore, four aft) plus four pusher propellers; turbine-electric hybrid genset (turbine-driven generator + battery + electric motor distribution). Modular cargo pod system swappable without aircraft contact.
sources: Elroy Air press releases and technical white papers 2019–2024.
USAF Agility Prime program disclosures.
FAA filings related to autonomous cargo operations.

creator: Sikorsky Aircraft (Lockheed Martin) / Boeing
disclosure_citation: Sikorsky-Boeing SB>1 Defiant first flight 2019-03-21 at West Palm Beach FL (U.S. Army Future Vertical Lift / Joint Multi-Role Technology Demonstrator program). Predecessor Sikorsky X2 first flight 2008-08-27. Sikorsky holds extensive coaxial-rigid-rotor patent estate.
ip_citations: Sikorsky X2 / Defiant patent estate, multiple US filings 2005–2024
U.S. Patents on rigid coaxial rotor technology
prior_art_notes: Sikorsky-Boeing Defiant establishes prior art for: (1) modern rigid coaxial counter-rotating rotor design overcoming retreating-blade-stall speed limits, (2) compound rotorcraft architecture (lift rotor + auxiliary cruise propulsion). Although not eVTOL, the architectural patterns directly anticipate any modern compound-rotorcraft eVTOL (Jaunt Journey, Karem AR40) claiming coaxial-plus-pusher novelty.
propulsion_details: Two coaxial counter-rotating rigid main rotors (descended from Sikorsky's X2 technology) for primary lift; rear pusher propeller for forward thrust. Rigid rotor design enables Advanced Blade-Tip Mach Number (ABMC) operation past conventional helicopter speed limits.
sources: Sikorsky / Boeing JMR-TD program disclosures.
Sikorsky X2 technology white papers, AHS / VFS Forums 2008–2024.
U.S. Army Future Vertical Lift program reports.

creator: Sabrewing Aircraft Company
disclosure_citation: Sabrewing Aircraft Rhaegal-A unveiled April 2019; sub-scale prototype hover trials 2020. Designed for 4,500 lb (2,040 kg) cargo capacity in unmanned configuration. Documented in Sabrewing technical materials and FAA filings.
ip_citations: Sabrewing patent estate, US filings 2018-2024
prior_art_notes: Sabrewing Rhaegal-A establishes US prior art for heavy-cargo (2-ton class) hybrid-electric eVTOL with ducted-fan architecture. Distinct from Elroy Air Chaparral by larger payload and ducted-fan rather than open-rotor lift. Anticipates other heavy-cargo eVTOL claims filed post-2019.
propulsion_details: Four ducted lift fans plus one pusher cruise fan; hybrid-electric powertrain with diesel genset for long-range operation.
sources: Sabrewing Aircraft technical materials 2019-2024.
FAA Part 23 / Special Conditions filings.

control_architecture: distributed control allocation across 36 fans, fly-by-wire
creator: Lilium GmbH
disclosure_citation: Lilium Jet 5-seat prototype unveiled and first hover flight 2019-05-16 at Manching; 7-seat production design unveiled 2021-03-30. Disclosures continued through Lilium SPAC merger Form S-4 2021-09 and subsequent NASDAQ filings until bankruptcy 2024-10.
ip_citations: Lilium GmbH patent estate including EP3401214, US10717543, multiple ducted-fan filings 2017–2023
prior_art_notes: Lilium Jet establishes prior art for the high-multiplicity ducted-fan-array eVTOL: 36 small embedded ducted fans tilting in flap-segment groupings to provide both lift and cruise. Architecturally a hyper-distributed extension of Bell X-22 (4 tilt-ducts) and Doak VZ-4 (2 tilt-ducts). Although Lilium filed for bankruptcy in October 2024, the patent estate (now in receivership / likely auctioned) covers specific implementations of the 36-fan ducted-array geometry and EASA SC-VTOL certification basis approaches. The fundamental architecture (multiple ducted fans in a tilting flap arrangement) is anticipated by Bell X-22 (1966).
propulsion_details: Thirty-six embedded electric ducted fans (24 on canard, 12 on main wing) tilting in flap segments; lithium-ion batteries; full DEP architecture with no separate cruise propulsor (ducted fans provide both lift and cruise thrust).
sources: Lilium Jet technical white paper 2021 (Munich, public download).
Lilium GmbH SPAC merger Form S-4 SEC filing 2021.
Wendel et al. 'Lilium Jet propulsion architecture' technical papers 2020–2022.

creator: Jaunt Air Mobility LLC (Montreal, Canada / Texas, USA) — subsidiary of AIRO Group; licensed Carter Aviation SR/C technology
disclosure_citation: Jaunt Air Mobility (founded 2019, headquartered in Montreal with Texas operations, a subsidiary of the AIRO Group) unveiled the Journey eVTOL — a slowed-rotor compound electric VTOL using 'ROSA' (Reduced rOtor Speed Aircraft) technology licensed from Carter Aviation — in 2019; selected for the U.S. Air Force AFWERX Agility Prime programme 2020. Documented in Jaunt Air Mobility materials, AFWERX records, and AIRO Group SEC filings (NASDAQ: AIRO).
ip_citations: Jaunt Air Mobility ROSA patents (licensed Carter Aviation SR/C technology), US filings 2019-2024
prior_art_notes: Jaunt Air Mobility's Journey eVTOL establishes Canadian/US prior art for the slowed-rotor-compound electric VTOL — a single large rotor that spins fast for jump-takeoff then slows dramatically in cruise while a wing carries lift and electric pushers provide thrust ('ROSA', licensed from Carter Aviation). A distinct eVTOL architecture from the multirotor and tilt-rotor approaches. Establishes prior art for: (1) slowed-rotor-compound electric VTOL, (2) jump-takeoff stored-inertia rotor in an electric eVTOL, (3) the wing-offloads-slowed-rotor-in-cruise eVTOL configuration. Together with cartercopter (1998, the SR/C origin), karem-ar40 (2008, optimum-speed rotor), and overair-butterfly (2020, OSR eVTOL), comprehensively places slowed/variable-RPM-rotor compound eVTOL architecture in prior art. Adds another Canadian entry alongside Horizon Aircraft and the Avro Canada Avrocar heritage.
propulsion_details: An electric compound rotorcraft: a single large four-blade rotor that spins fast for vertical takeoff/landing (jump-takeoff, stored inertia), then is slowed to ~50 of nominal RPM in cruise while a fixed wing carries the lift and four wing-mounted electric pusher propellers provide forward thrust; lithium-ion battery. The eVTOL realization of Carter Aviation's SR/C slowed-rotor compound technology — distinct architecture from both the multirotor and the tilt-rotor eVTOL approaches.
sources: Jaunt Air Mobility Journey technical materials 2019-2024.
U.S. Air Force AFWERX Agility Prime contract records (Jaunt).
AIRO Group SEC filings (NASDAQ: AIRO).

creator: Volocopter GmbH
disclosure_citation: VoloCity production-design unveiling 2019-08-14 at Helsinki ITS World Congress; precursor VC-200 first manned flight 2013-11-17; first public manned demo at Singapore 2019-10-22. EASA SC-VTOL certification process actively pursued; targeted operations Paris 2024 / 2026 commercial rollouts.
ip_citations: Volocopter GmbH patent estate, descended from DE102010032217
Multiple patents on multirotor airframe and DEP for passenger eVTOL
prior_art_notes: Volocopter VoloCity is the production design descending from e-volo VC1 (2011, first manned multicopter flight). 18-rotor circular-ring multirotor architecture, EASA SC-VTOL certification basis. Establishes the German prior-art lineage from e-volo through Volocopter for circular-ring multirotor passenger eVTOL — foundational and predates EHang in manned-flight precedence.
propulsion_details: Eighteen brushless DC motors driving fixed-pitch propellers around a 9.0 m circular ring; lithium-ion battery pack with redundant DC bus.
sources: Volocopter press releases 2013–2024.
EASA SC-VTOL filings, public docket.
Vertical Flight Society Forum technical papers from Volocopter 2014–2024.

control_architecture: rider control with stabilization; wheel-hub-mounted tilting micro-turbojets for lift and translation
creator: Lazareth Auto-Moto (Annecy, France) / Ludovic Lazareth
disclosure_citation: Lazareth Auto-Moto (a French custom-vehicle maker) unveiled the LMV 496 'La Moto Volante' — a motorcycle whose four wheels each contain a JetCat micro-turbojet that swivels downward for VTOL flight — in September 2019; tethered hover demonstrations 2021. A French jet-turbine flying motorcycle / hover-bike. Documented in Lazareth materials and motoring/aviation press.
ip_citations: Lazareth Auto-Moto LMV 496 design patents, French / EU filings
prior_art_notes: The Lazareth LMV 496 establishes French prior art for the four-micro-turbojet flying motorcycle — a road motorcycle whose four wheels each house a tilting micro-turbojet, swiveling downward for VTOL flight. Establishes prior art for: (1) wheel-integrated tilting-turbojet VTOL drive, (2) the jet-turbine flying-motorcycle / drive+fly hover-bike hybrid. Together with jetpack-aviation-speeder (2021, jet-turbine hover-bike), hoversurf-s3 (2017, open-rotor hover-bike), aerofex-aero-x (2008), and malloy-aeronautics-hoverbike (2014, ducted-rotor), comprehensively places hover-bike / flying-motorcycle architecture in cross-national prior art. Adds another French entry alongside the Eurocopter X3 / Airbus RACER / Flying Whales / SNECMA / Cyrano / Verne French VTOL heritage.
propulsion_details: A motorcycle (the Lazareth LM 847 'Maсерати-V8' custom bike) whose four wheels each house a JetCat axial-flow micro-turbojet; on the ground it drives on the wheels, then the wheel hubs tilt the turbojets downward and the bike lifts off as a four-jet VTOL. A four-micro-turbojet hover-bike / flying motorcycle — the French entry in the personal-jet-VTOL design space.
sources: Lazareth Auto-Moto LMV 496 technical materials and press releases 2019-2024.
Motoring and aviation press coverage of the Lazareth flying motorcycle 2019-2021.

creator: Kitty Hawk Corporation / Larry Page
disclosure_citation: Kitty Hawk Heaviside publicly unveiled 2019-10-03 at TED Conference / Wired25 Festival; first manned flight 2019-09. Kitty Hawk Corporation (funded by Larry Page) ceased operations 2022-09-21. Documented in Kitty Hawk press releases and the public technical paper 'Heaviside: A Single-Pilot eVTOL Aircraft' (Vertical Flight Society, 2020).
ip_citations: Kitty Hawk Corporation patent estate, US filings 2015-2022 — assigned to various successor entities post-shutdown
prior_art_notes: Kitty Hawk Heaviside establishes US prior art for ultralight-class single-pilot tilt-rotor eVTOL with explicit low-noise design optimization. Although Kitty Hawk Corporation ceased operations 2022, the Heaviside disclosure is comprehensive — Larry Page-funded program with VFS Forum technical paper publication and Kitty Hawk's continued patent portfolio licensable through successor entities. Kitty Hawk also produced Cora (which became Wisk Aero after the Boeing JV, covered separately).
propulsion_details: Eight tilting electric prop-rotors mounted on twin booms (four per side); lithium-ion battery; designed for very-low-noise (38 dB at 1500 ft) urban operation; FAA Part 103 ultralight category targeting.
sources: Kitty Hawk Corporation press releases 2019-2022.
Vertical Flight Society Forum 76 paper, 'Heaviside,' 2020.
TED Talk archive, Page/Kitty Hawk presentations 2018-2020.

creator: Pipistrel (Slovenia; acquired by Textron 2022)
disclosure_citation: Pipistrel (a Slovenian light-aircraft maker, the manufacturer of the Velis Electro — the first type-certified electric aeroplane) unveiled the Nuuva V300 hybrid VTOL cargo aircraft 2019-10-15; Pipistrel acquired by Textron 2022-04. The Nuuva V300 is intended as a heavy-cargo lift+cruise hybrid-electric eVTOL. Documented in Pipistrel/Textron materials and EASA filings.
ip_citations: Pipistrel / Textron patent estate, multiple Slovenian / EU / US filings 2015-2024
prior_art_notes: Pipistrel Nuuva V300 establishes Slovenian prior art for the hybrid-electric heavy-cargo eVTOL — eight electric lift rotors plus a single combustion-driven cruise pusher, designed for ~460 kg cargo over ~2,500 km range. Adds Slovenia (SI) to the global eVTOL OEM map. Pipistrel's parent company is Textron (also owner of Bell Textron) — the Pipistrel-Textron-Bell axis creates an integrated tilt-rotor + hybrid-cargo + electric-fixed-wing eVTOL family. Together with elroy-air-chaparral, sabrewing-rhaegal-a, wingcopter-198, pyka-pelican, amsl-vertiia, autoflight-prosperity-i, dji-flycart-30, and orb-aerospace-nomad, comprehensively places hybrid-electric cargo eVTOL architecture in commercial prior art across nine national/regional lineages.
propulsion_details: Eight electric lift rotors mounted on twin booms (four per side) plus a single combustion-driven pusher cruise propeller (a Rotax 915iS engine); designed for ~460 kg cargo payload and ~2,500 km range. Pipistrel's specific architectural choice — eight lift + single combustion-pusher — was selected for fuel-burn efficiency in cruise. Adds Slovenia (SI) to the global eVTOL OEM footprint and is now under Textron ownership alongside Bell.
sources: Pipistrel Nuuva V300 technical materials 2019-2024.
Textron acquisition of Pipistrel, 2022-04 disclosures.
EASA SC-VTOL public docket (Pipistrel).

creator: Volocopter GmbH (Bruchsal, Germany)
disclosure_citation: Volocopter VoloDrone publicly demonstrated 2019-10-30 at the Volocopter facility in Bruchsal, Germany — a heavy-cargo unmanned variant of the Volocopter 18-rotor multirotor architecture. Subsequently demonstrated for DB Schenker cargo logistics and at the Volocopter Hamburg test campaign. Documented in Volocopter press materials and EASA filings.
ip_citations: Volocopter GmbH patent estate (shared with VoloCity), German / EU / PCT filings
prior_art_notes: Volocopter VoloDrone establishes German prior art for the cargo-variant-of-passenger-multirotor pattern — unmanned heavy-cargo operations on the same 18-rotor airframe as the VoloCity passenger eVTOL, with up to 200 kg payload. Establishes prior art for: (1) shared-airframe passenger/cargo eVTOL family architecture, (2) underslung cargo and swappable container variants of a multirotor passenger eVTOL. Adds the cargo dimension to the Volocopter prior-art lineage (alongside the passenger volocopter-vc1 / volocopter-volocity).
propulsion_details: Eighteen brushless DC motors on a circular ring (same multirotor architecture as the VoloCity passenger eVTOL, but in unmanned cargo configuration) carrying a payload up to 200 kg under a slung load or in a swappable cargo container; battery-electric. The cargo variant of the Volocopter passenger eVTOL platform — establishes the cargo-variant-of-passenger-platform pattern that Wisk, Eve, and others may follow.
sources: Volocopter VoloDrone press materials and demonstrations 2019-2024.
DB Schenker / Volocopter cargo logistics partnership records.

control_architecture: redundant fly-by-wire
creator: Beta Technologies
disclosure_citation: Beta Technologies / Kyle Clark publicly unveiled Alia-250 design 2020-03-10; first hover flight 2020-05; first full transition flight 2021-04; multiple cross-country flights 2022–2024 publicly documented.
ip_citations: Beta Technologies patent estate 2018–2024 in CPC B64C 29/00 / B64D 27/24
prior_art_notes: Beta Alia-250 is the canonical commercial lift+cruise eVTOL: lift rotors stop and shut down in cruise, with a separate pusher providing forward propulsion. Architecturally simpler than tilt-rotor (no transition envelope coupling between lift and cruise), at cost of cruise drag from stopped lift rotors. Establishes prior art for: (1) production-scale lift+cruise architecture with mode-shutdown transition, (2) coaxial lift-pair geometry with single pusher cruise. Cited extensively by NASA Langley as the lift+cruise reference design.
propulsion_details: Four lift rotors mounted on dual booms (two coaxial pairs) for hover; single pusher propeller aft for cruise. All electric BLDC drive.
sources: Beta Technologies press releases 2020–2024.
Vertical Flight Society Forum technical papers from Beta 2021–2023.
FAA Part 23 / Special Conditions for VTOL filings.

creator: Manta Aircraft S.A. (Lugano, Switzerland / Varese, Italy)
disclosure_citation: Manta Aircraft ANN2 design publicly unveiled April 2020 at AERO Friedrichshafen; sub-scale prototype hover testing 2021. Manta Aircraft founded 2018 by Lucas Marchesini (Italian-Swiss aerospace engineer).
ip_citations: Manta Aircraft patent estate, Italian / Swiss / EU filings 2018-2024
prior_art_notes: Manta Aircraft ANN2 is the lead Italian-Swiss commercial eVTOL — hybrid-electric tilt-rotor with twin-fuselage geometry. Adds Italian prior-art lineage for hybrid-electric eVTOL alongside Leonardo AW609 (production tilt-rotor) and complements the Slovak (Klein Vision, AeroMobil) and Dutch (PAL-V) drive+fly Central European base.
propulsion_details: Eight tilting electric prop-rotors and one pusher cruise propeller; hybrid-electric (combustion genset + battery) for ~600 km range. Twin-fuselage geometry distinct from competitors.
sources: Manta Aircraft press releases 2018–2024.
AERO Friedrichshafen 2020 unveiling materials.
EASA SC-VTOL engagement records.

creator: Manna Drone Delivery Ltd
disclosure_citation: Manna Drone Delivery Ltd founded 2018 in Dublin by Bobby Healy; operational food delivery service launched September 2020 in Oranmore, Galway; expanded to multiple Irish towns and UK 2021-2024. Documented in Irish Aviation Authority (IAA) operational approvals and Manna press materials.
ip_citations: Manna Drone Delivery patent estate, Irish / EU filings
prior_art_notes: Manna Air Delivery establishes Ireland's lead commercial drone-delivery OEM with operational deployment 2020-2024 across Irish towns. Distinctive: tethered cargo drop without landing (architectural variant of cargo delivery distinct from DJI FlyCart's set-down approach). Adds Ireland to the Western European eVTOL ecosystem.
propulsion_details: Quad-rotor multirotor optimized for low-altitude (< 80 m) urban delivery operations; lithium-ion battery; tethered cargo bay drops payload at delivery point without landing.
sources: Manna Drone Delivery press releases 2020-2024.
Irish Aviation Authority operational approvals public record.
Reuters and Irish Times coverage 2020-2024.

creator: Klein Vision s.r.o (Nitra, Slovakia)
disclosure_citation: Klein Vision AirCar Prototype 1 first flight test 2020-10; first inter-airport flight 2021-06-28 (Nitra to Bratislava). Slovak Transport Authority issued Certificate of Airworthiness 2022-01-26 — the world's first roadable-aircraft type certificate. Klein Vision founded by Stefan Klein.
ip_citations: Klein Vision patent estate, Slovak / EU / PCT filings 2018–2024
prior_art_notes: Klein Vision AirCar holds the world's first type-certified roadable-aircraft certificate (Slovak Transport Authority, 2022-01-26). Establishes Slovak prior-art lineage and certified-product anchor for the drive+fly transformer category. Not VTOL but architecturally adjacent — many drive+fly eVTOL designs (ASKA A5, AeroMobil 4.0) build on the same retractable-wing transformer architecture pioneered in road-legal aviation by Klein.
propulsion_details: Conventional fixed-wing aircraft with retractable wings that fold into rear fuselage; 1.6 L BMW engine drives both road wheels and rear pusher propeller; transitions from car to aircraft in 2 minutes 15 seconds via push-button mechanism. Not strictly VTOL — STOL — but establishes drive+fly transformer prior art.
sources: Klein Vision press releases 2020–2024.
Slovak Transport Authority Certificate of Airworthiness, 2022-01-26.
Klein, Stefan. AirCar technical white paper, 2021.

creator: Eve Holding Inc (Embraer S.A. spinoff)
disclosure_citation: Eve UAM Solutions launched as Embraer subsidiary 2020-10-08; spun off as standalone Eve Holding via SPAC merger 2022-05-09 (NYSE: EVEX). Aircraft design publicly unveiled 2022; first prototype unveiled 2024.
ip_citations: Eve / Embraer patent estate, multiple Brazilian, US, and PCT filings
prior_art_notes: Eve Air Mobility is Brazil's lead commercial eVTOL, backed by Embraer's 100+-year aerospace heritage. The 8+1 lift+cruise architecture is architecturally similar to Beta Alia and AutoFlight Prosperity I; establishes Brazilian / Latin American prior-art lineage for commercial lift+cruise eVTOL. Eve has the deepest commercial aerospace certification experience among any eVTOL company by virtue of Embraer parentage.
propulsion_details: Eight lift rotors (four on each side, mounted on dual booms) for hover; single pusher propeller aft for cruise. Lithium-ion battery.
sources: Embraer / Eve Holding 20-F SEC filings 2022–2024.
Eve Air Mobility press releases 2020–2024.
ANAC (Agência Nacional de Aviação Civil) eVTOL certification framework filings.

creator: Overair Inc (Santa Ana, California) — Karem Aircraft spinoff; partnered with Hanwha Systems (Korea)
disclosure_citation: Overair (spun out of Karem Aircraft in 2020, with investment from Hanwha Systems of South Korea) unveiled the Butterfly eVTOL — a tilt-rotor eVTOL using Karem's 'Optimum Speed Tilt-Rotor' (OSTR) technology (large slow-turning prop-rotors with continuously-variable RPM) — in October 2020. Documented in Overair materials, Hanwha Systems disclosures, and U.S. Air Force AFWERX Agility Prime records.
ip_citations: Overair / Karem Aircraft OSTR / OSR patents, multiple US filings 2008-2024
prior_art_notes: Overair's Butterfly establishes prior art for the optimum-speed tilt-rotor eVTOL — a tilt-rotor eVTOL with four very large, slow-turning prop-rotors whose RPM is continuously varied with flight regime ('OSTR', from Karem Aircraft's OSR technology), giving low disk loading for quiet, efficient hover. Distinct from the small-fast-prop tilt-rotor eVTOLs (Joby S4, TCab E20) by the large-slow-prop approach. Establishes prior art for: (1) continuously-variable-RPM tilt-rotor in an eVTOL, (2) very-low-disk-loading large slow prop-rotors for quiet hover, (3) the OSR-applied-to-eVTOL configuration. Hanwha Systems (Korea) partnership ties this to the Korean eVTOL ecosystem (Hyundai Supernal, Plana). Together with karem-ar40 (2008, OSTR origin), cartercopter (1998), and jaunt-air-mobility (2019), comprehensively places variable-RPM / slowed-rotor / optimum-speed-rotor eVTOL architecture in prior art.
propulsion_details: A tilt-rotor eVTOL with four very large, slow-turning prop-rotors (low disk loading for quiet hover and high efficiency) whose RPM is continuously varied as a function of flight regime ('Optimum Speed Tilt-Rotor', from Karem Aircraft's OSR technology); the rotors tilt from vertical to horizontal for cruise; electric distributed propulsion. Distinct from the small-fast-prop tilt-rotor eVTOLs (Joby S4) by the large-slow-prop optimum-speed-rotor approach.
sources: Overair Butterfly technical materials 2020-2024.
Hanwha Systems (Korea) Overair investment disclosures.
U.S. Air Force AFWERX Agility Prime contract records (Overair).
Karem Aircraft / Overair OSR / OSTR patents, USPTO.

creator: AMSL Aero Pty Ltd (Bankstown, Australia)
disclosure_citation: AMSL Aero Vertiia design publicly unveiled March 2021; sub-scale prototype hover testing 2022; first hover flight 2023-02-15. AMSL Aero founded 2017 by Andrew Moore (former CSIRO and Boeing engineer) and Siobhan Lyndon. Targeted at hydrogen-fuel-cell-powered eVTOL ambulance and regional connectivity missions.
ip_citations: AMSL Aero patent estate, Australian and PCT filings
prior_art_notes: AMSL Aero Vertiia is Australia's lead commercial eVTOL with a distinctive commitment to hydrogen-fuel-cell propulsion for 1,000 km range — addressing the energy-density limitation of pure-battery eVTOL. Establishes Australian prior-art lineage for hydrogen-eVTOL and for long-range regional eVTOL distinct from short-range urban air taxi. Anticipates other hydrogen-fuel-cell eVTOL claims (e.g., Joby's post-acquisition H2 program, Airbus ZEROe rotorcraft).
propulsion_details: Eight lift propellers and two pusher cruise propellers; hydrogen fuel cells provide electrical power (with backup battery) — distinct architectural commitment to hydrogen as primary energy source for range extension to 1,000 km.
sources: AMSL Aero press releases 2021–2024.
Australian Civil Aviation Safety Authority (CASA) eVTOL framework filings.
Vertical Flight Society Forum 2023 papers from AMSL.

control_architecture: fly-by-wire stabilization; rider steers; vectored jet-turbine thrust for lift and translation
creator: JetPack Aviation (Los Angeles, California) / David Mayman
disclosure_citation: JetPack Aviation (founded by David Mayman, who also developed the JB-series jetpacks worn over the Statue of Liberty in 2015) unveiled the Speeder — a turbine-powered VTOL flying motorcycle — in April 2021; P1.5 prototype flight tests 2022-2024; awarded U.S. Air Force AFWERX and U.S. Army contracts. Documented in JetPack Aviation materials and AFWERX contract records.
ip_citations: JetPack Aviation Speeder / JB-series patents, US filings 2010-2024
prior_art_notes: The JetPack Aviation Speeder establishes US prior art for the jet-turbine hover-bike — a motorcycle-style straddle VTOL with four vectored jet turbines and fly-by-wire stabilization, no exposed rotors. Establishes prior art for: (1) jet-turbine-powered hover-bike (distinct from open-rotor hover bikes), (2) vectored-turbine lift and translation control for a personal VTOL, (3) heavy-fuel turbine personal VTOL. JetPack Aviation also developed the JB-series jetpacks (turbojet jetpacks flown 2015-2024) — together with bell-rocket-belt (1961), williams-x-jet (1982), gravity-industries-jet-suit (2017), martin-aircraft-jetpack (2008), solotrek-xfv (2001), aerofex-aero-x (2008), hoversurf-s3 (2017), and the historical personal-platform anchors (Hiller VZ-1, de Lackner HZ-1, Chrysler VZ-6), comprehensively places personal-VTOL platform and hover-bike architecture in deep prior art across rocket / ducted-fan / turbofan / turbojet / open-rotor / jet-turbine variants.
propulsion_details: A motorcycle-style straddle vehicle with four jet turbines (kerosene/diesel/Jet-A capable) arranged so their thrust can be vectored for lift and translation; fly-by-wire stabilization (the rider just steers); no exposed rotors (turbojet/turbofan exhaust instead). A jet-turbine hover-bike — distinct from the open-rotor hover bikes (Aerofex, Hoversurf, Malloy) and from the personal jet-suit (Gravity, JetPack Aviation's own JB-series).
sources: JetPack Aviation Speeder / JB-series technical materials 2015-2024.
U.S. Air Force AFWERX and U.S. Army contract records (JetPack Aviation).
JetPack Aviation patent filings, USPTO.

control_architecture: triplex fly-by-wire with simplified vehicle operations
creator: Archer Aviation
disclosure_citation: Archer Aviation S-1 SEC filing 2021-06-10; Archer Midnight design publicly unveiled 2022-11-16; Maker demonstrator first hover flight 2021-12; first full transition 2023-06.
ip_citations: Archer Aviation patent estate, multiple filings 2020–2024 in CPC B64C 29/00
prior_art_notes: Archer Midnight is a hybrid lift+cruise / tilt-rotor architecture with six tilting and six lift-only propellers. Architecturally descends from the NASA GL-10 DEP tilt-wing concept and the XV-15 tilt-rotor, with the lift+cruise split providing lower hover-prop disk loading at the cost of cruise drag from idle lift props. Disclosures in SEC filings make this a commons-grade entry. Establishes prior art for: (1) 6+6 hybrid tilt-and-lift-only DEP architecture, (2) feathering cruise props with fixed-cruise disk.
propulsion_details: Twelve electric motors driving twelve propellers: six wing-leading-edge tilting propellers (function as lift in hover and cruise propulsion) and six aft-mounted lift-only propellers (stop and feather in cruise).
sources: Archer Aviation S-1 (2021-06-10) and 10-K filings 2022–2025, SEC EDGAR.
Archer Aviation press releases including 2022-11-16 Midnight unveil.
Vertical Flight Society Forum 2023 technical paper from Archer engineering.

creator: CycloTech GmbH (Linz, Austria)
disclosure_citation: CycloTech GmbH (founded 2004 as D-Dalus successor, rebranded CycloTech 2020) unveiled the CruiseUp passenger eVTOL concept 2022-09; the CCY-5 technology demonstrator (a sub-scale flying testbed using six CycloRotors) achieved first untethered flight 2024-04-17 at Wels, Austria. CycloTech holds an extensive cycloidal-rotor patent estate.
ip_citations: CycloTech GmbH cycloidal-rotor patent estate, Austrian / EU / PCT filings 2004-2024
Building on the original Pescara cyclogiro patents (1928, expired)
prior_art_notes: CycloTech CruiseUp / CCY-5 is the modern production-oriented disclosure of cyclorotor (cyclogiro) eVTOL — six electric CycloRotors giving omnidirectional fully-actuated thrust via azimuthal blade-pitch modulation. Establishes Austrian prior art for: (1) passenger-scale cyclorotor eVTOL architecture, (2) electric CycloRotor propulsion with 360° in-plane thrust vectoring, (3) the omnidirectional fully-actuated cyclorotor control. Builds on the D-Dalus research demonstrator (2011) and the foundational Pescara cyclogiro patents (1928, long expired). Together with d-dalus and eth-omnicopter, comprehensively places cyclorotor and omnidirectional VTOL architecture in prior art.
propulsion_details: Six electric CycloRotors (cyclogiro / cycloidal propellers — barrel rotors with parallel airfoil blades whose pitch is cyclically varied as a function of azimuth) arranged so that thrust from each rotor can be vectored 360° in its plane; the combined system is fully actuated and omnidirectional. The modern production-oriented disclosure of cyclorotor eVTOL — distinct from the earlier D-Dalus research demonstrator (2011) by being a passenger-scale eVTOL development program.
sources: CycloTech GmbH technical white papers and press releases 2020-2024.
CycloTech CCY-5 first flight, Wels Austria, 2024-04-17.
Pescara cyclogiro patents (1928, foundational, expired).

control_architecture: autonomous flight management with pilot override; differential-thrust attitude allocation
creator: AeroHT (XPENG flying car subsidiary)
disclosure_citation: XPENG Voyager X2 publicly unveiled 2021-07-16 at XPENG Tech Day; first untethered manned flight 2022-10-10 at Dubai. AeroHT subsequently unveiled X3 (2023) and the modular Land Aircraft Carrier (2024). AeroHT holds Chinese patents on coaxial multirotor and modular drive+fly architecture.
ip_citations: AeroHT / XPENG patent estate, multiple Chinese filings
prior_art_notes: AeroHT (XPENG flying car subsidiary) is one of three lead Chinese passenger eVTOL programs (with EHang and AutoFlight). The X2 establishes Chinese prior art for coaxial-pair multirotor passenger eVTOL with autonomous + pilot-override flight management. AeroHT's subsequent X3 and modular Land Aircraft Carrier (2024) extend to drive+fly transformer geometry.
propulsion_details: Eight motors in coaxial pairs (four arms × 2 rotors) on a closed-loop frame; lithium-ion battery; full autonomous + pilot-override flight control.
sources: XPENG Tech Day 2021-07-16 announcement materials.
Reuters and South China Morning Post coverage of 2022-10-10 Dubai flight.
AeroHT product disclosures and Chinese patent filings.

creator: New Horizon Aircraft Ltd (Lindsay, Ontario, Canada)
disclosure_citation: New Horizon Aircraft (Canadian, founded 2013, public on NASDAQ as HOVR via SPAC merger 2024) unveiled the Cavorite X5 hybrid eVTOL in 2021; 50%-scale prototype first conventional flight 2023-11 and first full transition 2024-11. The Cavorite uses a 'fan-in-wing' design — fourteen lift fans embedded in the wings, covered by sliding panels in cruise so the aircraft flies as a conventional fixed-wing. Documented in Horizon Aircraft materials and SEC filings (named for H.G. Wells's anti-gravity material 'Cavorite').
ip_citations: New Horizon Aircraft Cavorite patents, Canadian / US filings 2013-2024
prior_art_notes: Horizon Aircraft Cavorite X5 establishes Canadian prior art for the modern 'fan-in-wing' hybrid eVTOL — fourteen electric lift fans embedded in the wing structure, covered by sliding panels in cruise so the wing becomes a clean conventional wing with a single pusher. Establishes prior art for: (1) modern panel-covered fan-in-wing eVTOL, (2) hybrid-electric fan-in-wing architecture, (3) the wing-as-clean-wing-in-cruise design. Directly descended in concept from vanguard-omniplane (1959) and ryan-xv-5-vertifan (1964). Together with these and lilium-jet (ducted-fan-array), aurora-lightningstrike-xv-24a (tilt-wing ducted-fan), and bell-nexus-4ex (tilt-duct), comprehensively places ducted-fan / fan-in-wing eVTOL architecture in prior art. Adds Canadian depth alongside Jaunt Air Mobility and the Avro Canada Avrocar heritage.
propulsion_details: A fixed-wing aircraft with fourteen electric lift fans embedded in the wing structure (seven per wing), covered by sliding panels that close in cruise so the wing becomes a clean conventional wing and a single rear pusher provides thrust; a turbine-genset-electric hybrid powertrain. The modern 'fan-in-wing' eVTOL — directly descended in concept from the Vanguard Omniplane (1959) and Ryan XV-5 Vertifan (1964) fan-in-wing VTOL programs. Adds Canadian eVTOL depth.
sources: New Horizon Aircraft Cavorite X5 technical materials 2021-2024.
New Horizon Aircraft SEC filings (NASDAQ: HOVR).

creator: MACA Industries (Toulouse, France) / Michaël Carchidi
disclosure_citation: MACA Industries (founded 2019 in Toulouse, France) unveiled the S11 Carcopter — a hydrogen-fuel-cell racing eVTOL — at the September 2021 Munich IAA Mobility show. Designed for an envisioned hydrogen racing league. Documented in MACA materials and aerospace press.
ip_citations: MACA Industries patent estate, French / EU / PCT filings 2019-2024
prior_art_notes: MACA Carcopter establishes French prior art for the hydrogen-fuel-cell racing eVTOL — distinct from Alauda Airspeeder (AU, battery-electric racing) by the hydrogen powertrain. Together with airspeeder (AU) and amsl-vertiia (AU, hydrogen passenger), places hydrogen and racing eVTOL architecture in cross-national prior art. Adds another French entry to the deep French VTOL heritage (SNECMA Coléoptère, Eurocopter X3, Airbus RACER, Flying Whales, Cornu, Oehmichen, d'Amécourt, Lazareth, Airbus Vahana, Airbus Pop.Up, Verne).
propulsion_details: An eight-rotor racing multirotor (four pylons, each carrying a coaxial pair) with a Formula-1-inspired single-seat composite cockpit; hydrogen fuel cells with a small lithium-ion buffer battery; designed for high-speed pylon racing in an envisioned hydrogen-fuelled racing league. Distinct from Alauda Airspeeder (Australian battery-electric racing eVTOL) by the hydrogen powertrain.
sources: MACA Industries S11 Carcopter technical materials and IAA Mobility 2021 unveiling.
MACA Industries press materials 2021-2024.

creator: Aerofugia (Geely Holding Group eVTOL subsidiary)
disclosure_citation: Geely Holding Group launched Aerofugia subsidiary 2021-09-14 in Chengdu; AE200 design publicly unveiled at Beijing Air Show 2022-02-10; sub-scale prototype hover 2023-04. Geely also acquired Terrafugia (US) 2017-11 and uses combined IP base for AE200.
ip_citations: Aerofugia / Geely patent estate, Chinese filings
Terrafugia (acquired) prior US patent estate
prior_art_notes: Geely Aerofugia AE200 is China's third major commercial passenger eVTOL (with EHang and AutoFlight). Distinctive: Geely's parallel ownership of Terrafugia (US flying car company since 2017) provides cross-Pacific patent integration. Establishes additional Chinese tilt-rotor prior-art lineage alongside TCab E20.
propulsion_details: Six tilting electric prop-rotors in a 4+2 configuration similar to Joby S4 and TCab E20; lithium-ion battery; lithium-ion solid-state battery development partnership with sister Geely brands.
sources: Aerofugia / Geely press releases 2021-2024.
Beijing Air Show 2022 unveiling materials.
Geely Holding Group investor disclosures.

creator: Airbus Helicopters (Donauwörth, Germany) / Airbus Defence and Space
disclosure_citation: Airbus CityAirbus NextGen design publicly unveiled 2021-09-21 at Airbus Summit; first scaled prototype hover 2024-09. EASA SC-VTOL type-certification process initiated 2022. Predecessor CityAirbus demonstrator first hover 2019-05-03.
ip_citations: Airbus Helicopters patent estate, multiple EU / US / PCT filings
prior_art_notes: Airbus CityAirbus NextGen is the production-track lift+cruise successor to the Vahana research program. Establishes German industrial prior-art lineage for production-scale lift+cruise passenger eVTOL with V-strut lift-rotor mounting geometry distinct from boom-mounted competitors (Beta Alia, Eve, Wisk). Airbus's industrial certification scale and EASA insider position make this a particularly important commons-grade entry.
propulsion_details: Eight lift propellers on V-shaped struts plus two pusher cruise propellers aft on a fixed-wing airframe; lithium-ion battery.
sources: Airbus Helicopters press releases 2019–2024.
Airbus Summit 2021 unveiling materials.
EASA SC-VTOL public docket.

creator: Vertical Aerospace Ltd (Bristol, United Kingdom)
disclosure_citation: Vertical Aerospace VX4 unveiled 2021-09-29 at SPAC announcement / investor presentation; first tethered hover 2022-09-26; first untethered transition 2024-12. SPAC merger with Broadstone Acquisition (NYSE: EVTL) completed 2021-12. Lead investors include Microsoft / Avolon / American Airlines / Honeywell / Rolls-Royce.
ip_citations: Vertical Aerospace patent estate, multiple UK, US, PCT filings 2019–2024
prior_art_notes: Vertical Aerospace VX4 is the United Kingdom's lead commercial eVTOL — the post-Hawker British vectored/tilt VTOL inheritance translated into electric propulsion. The 4+4 tilt-and-lift hybrid is architecturally between Joby S4 (6 tilt) and Archer Midnight (6+6 hybrid). Establishes UK prior-art lineage for commercial tilt-rotor eVTOL; Rolls-Royce propulsion subsystem involvement provides direct industrial-scale supplier coverage.
propulsion_details: Four wing-tip-mounted tilting electric propellers and four boom-mounted lift-only propellers; a 4+4 tilt-and-lift hybrid configuration. Lithium-ion battery; Honeywell flight controls; Rolls-Royce electric propulsion subsystems.
sources: Vertical Aerospace 20-F SEC filings 2021–2024.
Vertical Aerospace press releases including 2021-09-29 SPAC announcement.
Honeywell Avionics and Rolls-Royce eVTOL-program technical disclosures.

creator: Honda Motor Company / Honda Aircraft Company
disclosure_citation: Honda Motor Company eVTOL program publicly disclosed 2021-09-30 at 'Honda Dream Loop' announcement; design materials and target specifications subsequently disclosed in Honda investor briefings 2022–2024. Targeted commercial operation 2030.
ip_citations: Honda Motor Company eVTOL patent estate, Japanese / US filings
prior_art_notes: Honda eVTOL establishes prior art for hybrid-turbine-electric passenger eVTOL — Honda's specific architecture decision is to use a gas-turbine genset (leveraging HondaJet experience) for range extension. Anticipates other turbine-hybrid eVTOL claims (Elroy Air Chaparral, AMSL Aero Vertiia variants).
propulsion_details: Hybrid-electric powerplant with gas-turbine genset + battery (Honda leverages HondaJet turbine experience). Eight lift rotors and one or two cruise propellers. Distinctive commitment to gas-turbine-hybrid for 400+ km range, distinct from pure-battery short-range eVTOL.
sources: Honda 'Dream Loop' announcement, 2021-09-30.
Honda Motor Company investor disclosures 2021–2024.
Honda Aircraft Company technical materials.

control_architecture: fly-by-wire; three-mode (floating / foiling / flying) transition; blown-wing high-lift control
creator: REGENT Craft Inc (Rhode Island, USA) — founded by Boeing / Aurora Flight Sciences alumni
disclosure_citation: REGENT Craft (founded 2020 by Billy Thalheimer and Mike Klinker, ex-Aurora Flight Sciences / Boeing) unveiled the Viceroy seaglider 2021-10; quarter-scale prototype first flight 2022-08; full-scale Viceroy prototype rollout / first sea trials 2024-2025. The seaglider operates in three modes — floating (hull), foiling (hydrofoils), and flying (wing-in-ground-effect, ~9 m altitude). Documented in REGENT technical materials, U.S. Marine Corps / Navy contracts, and patent filings.
ip_citations: REGENT Craft patent estate, multiple US and PCT filings 2020-2024
prior_art_notes: REGENT Viceroy is the foundational modern disclosure of the electric wing-in-ground-effect (WIG) 'seaglider' — a distributed-electric-propulsion craft that transitions floating → hydrofoiling → ground-effect flight, operating a few meters above water. Establishes prior art for: (1) electric WIG / ekranoplan-class craft with distributed blown-wing propulsion, (2) the hull/foil/flight three-mode transition, (3) retractable-hydrofoil takeoff for a winged aircraft. While not vertical-takeoff in the classical sense, it is eVTOL-adjacent (the corpus scope covers ground-effect hybrids with VTOL-like operations) and shares DEP blown-wing architecture with eVTOL. Anticipates other electric-WIG and seaglider claims (a growing segment — also pursued by Wigetworks AirFish and others).
propulsion_details: Twelve distributed electric propellers along the wing leading edge (blown-wing high-lift for takeoff) plus retractable hydrofoils for the foiling phase; lithium-ion battery. The craft takes off from water by hydrofoiling up to speed, then transitions to wing-in-ground-effect (WIG) flight a few meters above the surface — a distinct VTOL-adjacent architecture (vertical hull-to-foil-to-flight transition rather than vertical takeoff) using distributed electric propulsion.
sources: REGENT Craft technical white papers and press releases 2021-2025.
U.S. Marine Corps / Navy seaglider contract records.
REGENT Craft patent filings, USPTO.

creator: AIR Mobility Ltd (Pardes Hanna, Israel)
disclosure_citation: AIR ONE publicly unveiled 2021-10-04 in San Francisco; first untethered manned hover flight 2023-06-19 at AIR Pardes Hanna test site, Israel. AIR Mobility founded by Rani Plaut and Chen Rosen, 2018.
ip_citations: AIR Mobility patent estate, Israeli and PCT filings 2019–2024
prior_art_notes: AIR ONE is Israel's lead consumer-grade eVTOL — distinct from CityHawk (commercial ducted-fan) by addressing the personal-recreational segment. Lift+cruise architecture targeting Part 23 certification with consumer operability emphasis. Establishes Israeli prior-art lineage for consumer/recreational eVTOL distinct from passenger air taxi.
propulsion_details: Eight lift rotors (four on each of two booms) plus a single pusher for cruise; lithium-ion battery; designed to fold/store like a consumer-grade ultralight.
sources: AIR Mobility press releases 2021–2024.
Globes (Israeli business newspaper) coverage of 2023-06-19 flight test.
Vertical Flight Society Forum 2023 papers from AIR engineering.

creator: AutoFlight (Shanghai)
disclosure_citation: AutoFlight Prosperity I publicly unveiled 2021-12-13; first untethered hover flight 2022-04; full-scale prototype transition flight 2023-02-23 at Kunshan; cross-Pearl River Delta demonstration flight Hong Kong → Macau 2024-04-29. AutoFlight founded by Tian Yu (former co-founder of EHang).
ip_citations: AutoFlight (Shanghai) patent estate, multiple Chinese filings 2018–2024
prior_art_notes: AutoFlight Prosperity I is the leading Chinese lift+cruise eVTOL — architectural sibling to Beta Alia-250 with eight rather than four lift rotors. The 250 km Hong Kong-Macau demonstration flight (2024-04-29) is the longest eVTOL flight publicly documented as of mid-2024. Establishes Chinese prior-art lineage for lift+cruise architecture and supports EASA SC-VTOL certification basis dual-track with EHang's CAAC certification.
propulsion_details: Eight lift rotors mounted on dual booms (four per side) for hover; single push propeller aft for cruise. Lithium-ion battery bus.
sources: AutoFlight press releases 2021–2024.
China Daily and Xinhua coverage of 2024-04-29 Hong Kong-Macau flight.
AutoFlight technical white papers (Chinese and English editions).

creator: Doroni Aerospace (Fort Lauderdale, Florida) / Doron Merdinger
disclosure_citation: Doroni Aerospace (founded 2016 by Doron Merdinger) unveiled the H1 two-seat personal eVTOL in 2022; first crewed test flight 2023-05; received an FAA Special Airworthiness Certificate (Experimental) 2023; developing the H1-X for FAA Part 23 certification. Designed to fit in a two-car garage and operate from driveways under (intended) Part 103 ultralight or LSA rules. Documented in Doroni materials and FAA filings.
ip_citations: Doroni Aerospace patent estate, US filings 2017-2024
prior_art_notes: Doroni H1 establishes US prior art for the garage-storable enclosed-rotor two-seat personal eVTOL — eight ducted lift rotors housed within the airframe body (not on exposed booms), small fixed wings, car-like footprint, driveway operation. Establishes prior art for: (1) fully-enclosed-within-body lift rotors on a personal eVTOL, (2) garage-storable / driveway-operable two-seat personal eVTOL form factor. Together with pivotal-blackfly, jetson-one, lift-aircraft-hexa, alef-model-a, and the historical personal-platform anchors, comprehensively places small-personal eVTOL architecture in prior art.
propulsion_details: An enclosed two-seat personal eVTOL with eight ducted electric lift rotors (housed within the airframe body, not on exposed booms — a fully-enclosed-rotor design for safety and a car-like footprint), plus small fixed wings for cruise lift; lithium-ion battery; fly-by-wire with simplified controls. A garage-storable, driveway-operable two-seat enclosed-rotor eVTOL.
sources: Doroni Aerospace H1 / H1-X technical materials 2022-2024.
FAA Special Airworthiness Certificate dossier (Doroni H1).

creator: The ePlane Company (IIT Madras spinout)
disclosure_citation: The ePlane Company e200 unveiled 2022-08; first sub-scale prototype hover flight 2022-12; full-scale prototype rolled out 2023-09 at IIT Madras Research Park. Founded 2017 by Prof. Satya Chakravarthy. Awarded Anjani Mashelkar Inclusive Innovation Award 2023.
ip_citations: ePlane Company / IIT Madras patent estate, Indian and PCT filings
prior_art_notes: ePlane e200 is India's lead commercial eVTOL — IIT Madras research spinout with explicit design constraint for compact urban Indian airspace. Establishes Indian prior-art lineage for commercial lift+cruise eVTOL and the design philosophy of compact-footprint operation in dense urban environments. Targeted at DGCA (Directorate General of Civil Aviation, India) certification.
propulsion_details: Eight lift rotors mounted on twin booms for hover; single pusher for cruise; lithium-ion battery; specifically designed for the dense footprint of Indian urban airspace (compact rotor disc geometry).
sources: Chakravarthy, Satya. ePlane Company technical white papers, 2022–2024.
Indian DGCA eVTOL certification framework filings.
IIT Madras Research Park ePlane Company disclosures.

creator: Plana Aerospace Inc (Seongnam, South Korea)
disclosure_citation: Plana Aerospace (founded 2021 in South Korea by Braden Kim) unveiled the CP-01 hybrid-electric eVTOL in 2022; sub-scale prototype flight tests 2023-2024; targeting Korean (MOLIT) and FAA certification for regional air mobility. Documented in Plana materials and Korean Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport (MOLIT) records.
ip_citations: Plana Aerospace patent estate, Korean / US / PCT filings 2021-2024
prior_art_notes: Plana Aero CP-01 establishes Korean prior art for the hybrid-electric regional eVTOL — a lift+cruise eVTOL with a turbine-genset-electric hybrid powertrain for ~500 km range, targeting intercity routes. Distinct from the urban-air-taxi-focused Korean peer Hyundai Supernal (battery-electric, 100 km). Establishes Korean depth in the hybrid-electric eVTOL space alongside Honda (JP), Zuri (CZ), Elroy Air (US), and AMSL Vertiia (AU). Together with hyundai-supernal-sa2, the Hanwha-Systems-backed overair-butterfly, and (fictionally) space-sweepers-victory, deepens Korea's eVTOL footprint.
propulsion_details: A lift+cruise eVTOL with eight tilting electric lift rotors and a pusher cruise propeller, powered by a turbine-genset-electric hybrid powertrain for ~500 km range (distinguishing Plana from pure-battery Korean and global peers); designed for regional intercity routes. The Korean hybrid-electric regional-eVTOL entry.
sources: Plana Aerospace CP-01 technical materials 2022-2024.
Korean MOLIT (Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport) UAM programme records.

creator: Crisalion Mobility S.L. (Tecnalia Research and Innovation spinoff)
disclosure_citation: Crisalion Mobility Integrity design publicly unveiled 2022-09-08 at Madrid; sub-scale demonstrator hover 2023. Crisalion Mobility spun off from Tecnalia Research and Innovation (Basque Country research center). Documented in Crisalion press materials and Tecnalia research disclosures.
ip_citations: Tecnalia / Crisalion patent estate, Spanish and EU filings
prior_art_notes: Crisalion Mobility Integrity is Spain's lead commercial eVTOL — Tecnalia Research and Innovation (Basque Country) spin-off establishes Spanish industrial prior-art lineage for tilt-rotor distributed-electric passenger eVTOL. Adds Spain to the European eVTOL OEM map alongside Germany (Volocopter, Lilium, Wingcopter, Airbus CityAirbus), Italy (Leonardo, Manta), Netherlands (PAL-V), Slovakia (Klein Vision, AeroMobil), Czech Republic (Zuri), Sweden (Jetson).
propulsion_details: Eight tilting electric prop-rotors in a coaxial-pair configuration on an X-frame; lithium-ion battery; modular passenger/cargo configurations.
sources: Crisalion Mobility press releases 2022-2024.
Tecnalia Research and Innovation public technical disclosures.

creator: Alef Aeronautics Inc (San Mateo, California)
disclosure_citation: Alef Aeronautics Model A publicly unveiled 2022-10-19; received FAA Special Airworthiness Certificate (Experimental) 2023-06-12 — the first 'flying car' to receive an FAA airworthiness certificate. Alef (founded 2015) demonstrated a full-scale prototype driving and (briefly) lifting 2022-2023. Documented in Alef press materials, FAA filings, and Alef patent filings.
ip_citations: Alef Aeronautics patent estate, US filings 2015-2024
US Patent 11,167,839 and related — mesh-skin lift architecture
prior_art_notes: Alef Aeronautics Model A establishes US prior art for the rotors-under-mesh-skin drive+fly transformer — a road-legal car with eight electric lift rotors hidden under a perforated upper skin, taking off vertically through the mesh and then rotating the whole body (cabin gimballed level) to fly as a biplane-wing. The first 'flying car' to receive an FAA airworthiness certificate (2023). Establishes prior art for: (1) mesh-skin / perforated-surface lift-rotor concealment architecture, (2) whole-body-rotation drive+fly transition with gimballed cabin, (3) the car-body-as-biplane-wing configuration. Distinct from the folding-wing transformers (Klein Vision, AeroMobil, ASKA) and modular-capsule transformers (Airbus Pop.Up, XPENG AeroHT). Together with these and pal-v-liberty (autogyro transformer), comprehensively places drive+fly transformer architecture in prior art across folding-wing / modular-capsule / whole-body-rotation / autogyro variants.
propulsion_details: A two-seat road-legal car body whose entire upper surface is a mesh (perforated) skin under which eight electric lift rotors are mounted; the car drives on roads normally, then the whole vehicle rotates 90° (the cabin gimbals to stay level while the body becomes a 'biplane wing') for forward flight after vertical takeoff through the mesh skin. A distinct drive+fly transformer architecture — rotors-under-mesh-skin with whole-body rotation, unlike the folding-wing transformers (Klein Vision, AeroMobil) or modular-capsule transformers (Airbus Pop.Up, XPENG AeroHT).
sources: Alef Aeronautics press releases 2022-2024.
FAA Special Airworthiness Certificate dossier (Alef Model A), 2023-06-12.
Alef Aeronautics patent filings, USPTO (incl. US 11,167,839).

creator: TCab Tech (Shanghai TCab)
disclosure_citation: TCab Tech E20 sub-scale technology demonstrator first transition flight 2022-12-07 at Tianjin; full-scale E20 prototype unveiled 2023-09-13. TCab founded 2021 in Shanghai by Yon Wui Ng; technical leadership from former Volocopter and Lilium engineers.
ip_citations: TCab Tech patent estate, Chinese filings 2022–2024
prior_art_notes: TCab Tech E20 is China's lead tilt-rotor passenger eVTOL — architectural sibling to Joby S4 with the same 4+2 tilt-rotor configuration. Establishes Chinese-lineage prior art for the tilt-rotor electric eVTOL category, ensuring the architectural pattern is disclosed across at least four geographic prior-art bases (US, EU, China, Italy).
propulsion_details: Six tilting electric prop-rotors in a 4+2 configuration: four wing-mounted tilting and two stabilator-mounted tilting; lithium-ion battery.
sources: TCab Tech press releases and technical white papers 2022–2024.
Vertical Flight Society Forum 2023 papers including TCab Tech engineering.
South China Morning Post and Caixin coverage 2022–2024.

creator: SZ DJI Technology Co Ltd (Shenzhen)
disclosure_citation: DJI FlyCart 30 publicly unveiled 2023-08-16 in Shenzhen; CAAC type certification application 2023; commercial deliveries to Chinese and international markets 2024. DJI is the world's largest drone manufacturer and holds an extensive multirotor patent estate.
ip_citations: DJI patent estate, vast (>1000 patents) covering multirotor architectures, autopilot, motors
prior_art_notes: DJI FlyCart 30 is the entry of the world's largest drone manufacturer into the heavy-cargo eVTOL category. DJI's massive patent estate and operational scale (millions of multirotor flights/day globally) constitute foundational prior-art coverage for: (1) coaxial-pair multirotor manufacturing at scale, (2) autonomous multirotor cargo delivery, (3) consumer-priced multirotor architecture migration to commercial cargo class. Establishes Chinese prior-art lineage for heavy-payload multirotor cargo eVTOL with explicit Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) operational basis.
propulsion_details: Four-axis eight-rotor coaxial-pair multicopter (four arms, two motors per arm); designed for 30 kg cargo capacity over 16 km range; lithium-ion battery.
sources: DJI press releases and product disclosures 2023–2024.
CAAC type certification dossier (DJI FlyCart 30).
DJI patent estate, USPTO and CNIPA records.

creator: Sarla Aviation (Bangalore, India)
disclosure_citation: Sarla Aviation (founded 2023 in Bangalore by Adrian Schmidt, Rakesh Gaonkar, and Shivam Chauhan; named after Sarla Thakral, India's first woman pilot) unveiled the Shunya eVTOL air taxi in November 2023, with production targeted for ~2028 in Indian cities (Bangalore, Mumbai, Delhi, Pune); sub-scale prototype work 2024. Documented in Sarla materials and Indian DGCA engagement records.
ip_citations: Sarla Aviation patent estate, Indian / PCT filings 2023-2024
prior_art_notes: Sarla Aviation Shunya establishes additional Indian prior art for the urban-air-taxi multirotor eVTOL — a five-seat eVTOL designed for short hops in dense Indian cities, the second major Indian eVTOL OEM after ePlane. Establishes Indian depth alongside eplane-company-e200 (IN, lift+cruise) and the Indian fictional flight anchor (Krrish). Together with ehang-eh216 (CN), volocopter-volocity (DE), and the global multirotor air-taxi cluster, places urban-air-taxi multirotor eVTOL architecture in prior art across US, EU, China, India, Japan, Korea, and Brazil lineages.
propulsion_details: A four-passenger (plus pilot) eVTOL air taxi with eight electric lift rotors, designed for short urban hops in dense Indian cities; lithium-ion battery; intended to be priced competitively with premium ground ride-hailing. The second major Indian eVTOL OEM (after ePlane).
sources: Sarla Aviation Shunya technical materials 2023-2024.
Indian DGCA (Directorate General of Civil Aviation) UAM engagement records.

control_architecture: fly-by-wire airship control; electric vectored thrust for VTOL augmentation; lidar ballast management
creator: LTA Research and Exploration (Mountain View, CA) — funded by Sergey Brin
disclosure_citation: LTA Research Pathfinder 1 received FAA Special Airworthiness Certificate (Experimental) 2023-09; first untethered flight 2023-11-08 at Moffett Field, CA. LTA Research (funded by Google co-founder Sergey Brin since ~2016) builds rigid electric airships for humanitarian cargo and disaster relief. Documented in FAA filings and LTA Research materials.
ip_citations: LTA Research and Exploration patent estate, multiple US filings 2017-2024
prior_art_notes: LTA Research Pathfinder 1 establishes modern prior art for the electric rigid airship with vectored-thrust VTOL augmentation — buoyant helium lift augmented by distributed electric propellers for controlled vertical takeoff/landing without ground infrastructure. Establishes prior art for: (1) modern rigid electric airship architecture, (2) lidar-based ballast/lift management, (3) the buoyant-hybrid VTOL concept (static lift + vectored thrust). The corpus scope includes buoyant-hybrid craft with VTOL-like operations. Together with hybrid-air-vehicles-airlander (2012) and flying-whales-lca60t (2012), establishes the modern hybrid-airship / buoyant-VTOL prior-art base.
propulsion_details: Rigid airship (~124 m long) using helium for static lift, with twelve electric motor-driven propellers (vectorable) for propulsion and vertical-takeoff control augmentation; lidar-based ballast/lift management; carbon-fiber-and-titanium internal frame. A modern electric rigid airship — buoyant lift augmented by vectored electric thrust for controlled vertical takeoff and landing without ground infrastructure.
sources: LTA Research and Exploration technical materials and press releases 2023-2025.
FAA Special Airworthiness Certificate dossier (Pathfinder 1).
Pathfinder 1 first flight, Moffett Field CA, 2023-11-08.

creator: Supernal LLC (Hyundai Motor Group eVTOL subsidiary)
disclosure_citation: Hyundai Supernal S-A2 unveiled at CES 2024 (Las Vegas), 2024-01-09. Predecessor S-A1 design unveiled at CES 2020 (2020-01-07). Supernal LLC incorporated under Hyundai Motor Group, headquartered in Washington DC with engineering in Irvine CA, but the design lineage and corporate ownership are South Korean.
ip_citations: Hyundai Motor Group / Supernal patent estate, multiple US, KR, and PCT filings
prior_art_notes: Hyundai Supernal S-A2 is Korea's lead commercial eVTOL program, backed by Hyundai Motor Group's automotive industrial scale. The 4+4 tilt-rotor configuration is architecturally between Joby S4 (4+2) and tilt-wing designs. Establishes Korean prior-art lineage for tilt-rotor commercial eVTOL; targets FAA Part 23 certification and 2028 commercial operation.
propulsion_details: Eight tilting electric prop-rotors arranged in a 4+4 configuration (four wing-mounted, four stabilator-mounted); battery-electric with Hyundai Mobis battery integration.
sources: Supernal CES 2024 press materials, 2024-01-09.
Hyundai Motor Group investor disclosures.
FAA Part 23 / Special Conditions for VTOL filings.

aliases: 小鹏汇天 陆地航母
creator: AeroHT (XPENG flying car subsidiary)
disclosure_citation: XPENG AeroHT Land Aircraft Carrier publicly unveiled 2024-04-04 at Beijing Auto Show; production target 2026. Combines a road-legal 6x6 ground vehicle ('Mothership') containing a fold-out manned eVTOL drone.
ip_citations: AeroHT / XPENG patent estate, multiple Chinese filings
prior_art_notes: XPENG AeroHT Land Aircraft Carrier is the most ambitious modular drive+fly transformer architecture publicly disclosed: a separate ground vehicle that carries and deploys a manned eVTOL. Establishes Chinese prior-art lineage for modular vehicle-eVTOL architectures, distinct from integrated transformer-eVTOL designs (ASKA A5, AeroMobil) where one vehicle physically transforms. Anticipates future modular-carrier patent filings.
propulsion_details: Modular two-component system: (1) road-legal 6x6 hybrid-electric ground vehicle that carries (2) a fold-out single-pilot multirotor eVTOL deployed from the rear of the vehicle. The eVTOL has six rotors in a deployable wing arrangement.
sources: XPENG AeroHT Beijing Auto Show 2024-04-04 unveiling materials.
AeroHT / XPENG technical disclosures 2024.
Reuters and South China Morning Post coverage 2024-04-04.

creator: Airbus Helicopters / Clean Sky 2 (EU Horizon programme)
disclosure_citation: Airbus RACER first flight 2024-04-25 at Marignane, France; developed under the EU Clean Sky 2 / Clean Aviation programme (announced 2017, rolled out 2023, first flight 2024). Production-track successor to the Eurocopter X3 demonstrator. Documented in Airbus Helicopters / Clean Sky technical materials.
ip_citations: Airbus Helicopters RACER patent estate, EU and US filings 2017-2024
prior_art_notes: The Airbus RACER is the production-track European compound-helicopter demonstrator (first flight 2024). Refines the Eurocopter X3 architecture with a box-wing carrying lateral cruise/anti-torque propellers and an engine eco-mode (one engine shut down in cruise). Establishes prior art for: (1) box-wing compound-helicopter configuration, (2) eco-mode engine shutdown in compound rotorcraft cruise. Together with eurocopter-x3 (2010) and the Sikorsky compound-rotorcraft lineage, comprehensively places compound-rotorcraft architecture in 1957-2024 prior art.
propulsion_details: Single main rotor for lift, plus a 'box-wing' carrying two lateral propellers ('lateral rotors') for cruise thrust and anti-torque; two Safran Aneto-1X turboshafts with an eco-mode that can shut down one engine in cruise. Refines the X3 compound architecture with the box-wing for aerodynamic efficiency and noise reduction.
sources: Airbus Helicopters / Clean Sky 2 RACER technical materials 2017-2024.
Airbus press release, RACER first flight, 2024-04-25.
